Skip to main content ☮️ Save the World Inequality & Social Justice

Racial, gender, caste, and structural inequality perpetuate poverty, reduce overall societal wellbeing, and constrain economic output.

Inequality is not just an ethical issue — it's an economic one. The IMF has shown that more equal societies grow faster and more sustainably than unequal ones.
The richest 1%

own more than the bottom 50% combined
What Actually Works Legal Reform & Enforcement

Anti-discrimination laws only work if they're enforced. Strengthening legal frameworks and funding enforcement mechanisms creates accountability.
Targeted Community Investment

Directing resources to historically excluded communities addresses the compounding effects of past discrimination. Evidence from the U.S. Community Reinvestment Act shows this works.
Close Education & Healthcare Gaps

Ensuring equal access to quality education and healthcare breaks the intergenerational transmission of disadvantage.
Political Representation

Representation in decision-making bodies leads to policies that reflect the needs of diverse populations. Quotas and targeted recruitment programs can accelerate change.
Disaggregated Data Collection

You can't fix what you don't measure. Collecting and publishing data broken down by race, gender, income, and other factors makes invisible inequality visible.
How to Actually Do It
  1. Support fair housing policy and enforcement
  2. Advocate for diverse hiring practices and pay transparency
  3. Push for campaign finance reform to reduce concentrated power
  4. Support disaggregated data collection in public policy
  5. Fund organizations working on structural reform
 What Digital Makers Can Do

You design and build digital things. Here's how those skills apply.

 Individual

Audit your own products for bias. Who can't use what you built? Whose needs did you never consider? Practice inclusive design not as a checklist but as a discipline. Hire diversely. Pay equitably. Credit collaborators.

 Community

Offer pro bono work to civil rights organizations, legal aid societies, and community advocacy groups. Build tools that make it easier for marginalized communities to organize, document injustice, and access legal resources.

 Country

Build voter registration and election information tools that are accessible in every language your community speaks. Create open-source platforms for tracking policy impacts on different demographic groups. Make government data legible.

 World

Design tools and platforms that center the voices of people in the Global South rather than treating them as passive recipients. Contribute to projects that document human rights violations. Build technology that distributes power rather than concentrating it.

 Further Reading ACLU

Defending individual rights and liberties guaranteed by the Constitution.

 Equal Justice Initiative

Committed to ending mass incarceration and challenging racial and economic injustice.

 UN Women

Dedicated to gender equality and the empowerment of women.

 Oxfam Inequality Reports

Research and advocacy on the causes and consequences of extreme inequality.

Built with the Eddie Design System by Brad Frost.
